title: TC001-Open dashboard and access Add Class from quick action
description: Verifies the dashboard loads after login, shows core widgets, and the Add Class action navigates to the Add Class page.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5174
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5174", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Navigate to /login (http://localhost:5174/login) to reach the login form as the test step specifies.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5174/login", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert '/' in frame.url
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Real-Time Clock').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        assert '/class/add' in frame.url
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST FAILURE

ASSERTIONS:
- Login page did not render: page shows 0 interactive elements and a blank screenshot
- Email and password input fields not found on /login
- Log In button not available on the page, preventing authentication
- Dashboard could not be verified because login could not be performed
- SPA at http://localhost:5174/login failed to initialize after multiple wait attempts

title: TC002-Dashboard shows classes list after login
description: Verifies that the dashboard renders a visible list/section of classes after successful authentication.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5174
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5174", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Navigate to /login (use explicit navigate to http://localhost:5174/login as required by the test steps).
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5174/login", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert '/' in frame.url

        await expect(frame.locator('text=Class List').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)

    finally:

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST FAILURE

ASSERTIONS:
- Login page at http://localhost:5174/login did not render; no interactive elements (inputs/buttons) found.
- SPA content failed to load after two wait attempts; page appears blank.
- Dashboard could not be reached and authentication could not be performed; 'Classes' section and class list could not be verified.

title: TC024-Filter tasks by status Completed shows only completed tasks
description: Verifies that selecting Completed in the tasks status filter limits the task list to completed items.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5174
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5174", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Navigate to /login using the site's base URL (required explicit navigation step).
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5174/login", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert '/' in frame.url
        assert '/tasks' in frame.url
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Completed').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

TEST FAILURE

- Without a visible login form or navigation elements, the test cannot proceed to verify the tasks status filter behavior.
